items.title: Three-way data partition
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  allData["labeled dataset D"]
  trainPart["D_train: fit parameters"]
  valPart["D_val: model selection"]
  testPart["D_test: final evaluation"]
  allData --> trainPart
  allData --> valPart
  allData --> testPart
items.caption: Disjoint train, validation, and test sets — each row belongs to exactly one partition.

items.title: Development workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  splitStep["split data first"]
  fitTrain["fit on D_train"]
  evalVal["evaluate on D_val"]
  tuneLoop["tune hyperparameters"]
  lockTest["single evaluation on D_test"]
  splitStep --> fitTrain --> evalVal --> tuneLoop
  tuneLoop -->|"iterate"| fitTrain
  tuneLoop -->|"final model"| lockTest
items.caption: Validation supports repeated experiments; the test set is used once for an unbiased generalization estimate.

items.title: What each split measures
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  trainMetric["train error: fit quality"]
  valMetric["validation error: selection proxy"]
  testMetric["test error: sign-off estimate"]
  trainData["D_train used in fit"]
  valData["D_val never in fit"]
  testData["D_test never in fit"]
  trainData --> trainMetric
  valData --> valMetric
  testData --> testMetric
items.caption: Training metrics measure memorization on seen data; validation and test estimate performance on held-out labeled rows.

items.title: Split strategy choice
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  dataKind{"data structure?"}
  randomSplit["random stratified split"]
  timeSplit["temporal train/val/test"]
  groupSplit["group hold-out by user/patient"]
  dataKind -->|"i.i.d. tabular"| randomSplit
  dataKind -->|"time series / forecasting"| timeSplit
  dataKind -->|"repeated units per entity"| groupSplit
items.caption: The split method must match how unseen production examples will arrive — not every problem uses a random shuffle.
